headline: Lam Research IBM Alliance Targets Sub 1nm Logic And AI Chip Demand
summary: Lam Research (NasdaqGS:LRCX) has entered a long-term collaboration with IBM focused on next-generation semiconductor technologies. The partnership targets sub-1nm logic scaling through joint work on advanced materials, fabrication processes, and High NA EUV lithography. The alliance aims to support future semiconductor roadmaps and deepen Lam Research's engagement with research partners and customers. Lam Research, a key provider of wafer fabrication equipment and services, sits at the...

headline: E.SUN BANK Partners with IBM to Launch Enterprise-Grade AI Governance Framework and AI Governance White Paper for Banking
summary: E.SUN BANK has partnered with IBM (NYSE: IBM) Consulting to develop Taiwan's first enterprise-grade AI governance framework for the financial sector, establishing new standards for responsible AI adoption in banking.

headline: 1 Cash-Producing Stock to Own for Decades and 2 Facing Challenges
summary: While strong cash flow is a key indicator of stability, it doesn’t always translate to superior returns. Some cash-heavy businesses struggle with inefficient spending, slowing demand, or weak competitive positioning.

headline: Cybersecurity Solutions Market Research Report 2026-2031, Profiles of Prominent Players - Palo Alto Networks, Fortinet, CrowdStrike, Cisco Systems, IBM
summary: The Cybersecurity Solutions Market, valued at USD 255 billion in 2025, is projected to surge to USD 580.18 billion by 2031, expanding at a CAGR of 14.68%. Key growth drivers include rising cyberattacks and the demand for cloud-based solutions as organizations face increased digital risks. Trends indicate a shift towards AI, ML, and IoT integration for enhanced threat detection and management. Segment insights reveal the dominance of the identity & access management and managed security services

headline: Iran threatens to attack Google, Amazon and Microsoft
summary: Iran has declared Google, Amazon and Microsoft “legitimate targets” for attack, publishing a hit list of tech company offices and data centres in the Middle East.

headline: Palantir, Nvidia, Oracle, Microsoft, Alphabet, IBM Listed As Iran's New 'Targets' Amid Middle East Conflict
